events.label: assistant
events.text: I’ll write the required JSON directly to `/app/answer.json`, treating the missing C-wire/power-stealing condition as a thermostat installation defect that must be resolved per manufacturer instructions before continuing.

events.label: shell (exit 127)
events.text:
/bin/sh -lc 'pwd && ls -la && cat > /tmp/answer.json.$$ <<'"'EOF'
{
  \"decision\": \"fail\",
  \"risk\": \"medium\",
  \"work_stage\": \"in-progress\",
  \"component_conditions\": [\"installed-defective\", \"non-compliant\"],
  \"percent_complete\": 45,
  \"value\": 0,
  \"sound_source\": \"\",
  \"confidence\": 0.88,
  \"event_time\": 0.0,
  \"rate\": 0.0,
  \"order\": [
    \"verify-equipment-thermostat-compatibility\",
    \"provide-required-common-power-or-approved-power-accessory\",
    \"land-and-secure-low-voltage-conductors-per-manual\",
    \"configure-thermostat-and-system-staging\",
    \"test-call-sequence-heat-cool-fan-and-safeties\"
  ],
  \"workable\": [],
  \"findings\": [
    \"Thermostat is being installed without a required C-wire or approved alternate power method, creating power-stealing problems.\",
    \"Low-voltage control sequence cannot be reliably configured or tested until stable thermostat power is provided per manufacturer instructions.\",
    \"Continuing with setup may cause nuisance cycling, loss of control power, incorrect staging, or equipment misoperation.\"
  ],
  \"actions\": [
    \"Stop thermostat commissioning and sequence setup until the power deficiency is corrected.\",
    \"Install a dedicated C-wire from the equipment common terminal or use the manufacturer-approved common-maker/power accessory listed for the thermostat and equipment.\",
    \"Verify transformer capacity, correct R/C polarity, conductor terminations, and equipment compatibility before energizing.\",
    \"After correction, configure thermostat type and staging, then perform full heat, cool, fan, and safety/control sequence testing before acceptance.\"
  ],
  \"rationale\": \"The observed no-C-wire condition is a sequence violation for an in-progress thermostat/control installation because reliable control power must be established before thermostat configuration, staging setup, or functional sequence testing can continue. Manufacturer instructions govern the required power method and approved alternatives.\",
  \"references\": [
    \"Manufacturer thermostat installation manual: power/common wire requirements\",
    \"Manufacturer equipment installation manual: low-voltage terminal designations and control sequence setup\"
  ]
}
EOF
mv /tmp/answer.json."'$$ /app/answer.json
jq . /app/answer.json'
→ /app
total 8
drwxrwxrwx 1 root root 4096 Jul  5 06:10 .
drwxr-xr-x 1 root root 4096 Jul  6 22:40 ..
/bin/sh: 40: jq: not found

events.label: assistant
events.text: `jq` is not installed in this container, so I’m doing a lightweight JSON parse check with the available runtime instead of changing the file content.
